label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure this out. The question is asking what the author did when he met Mr. Lee. Let's go back to the passage. The story starts with the author, as a ten-year-old, walking with his mother. They stop to speak to Mr. Lee. The key part here is that the author says, "I knew I could see Mr. Lee any time around the neighborhood, so I didn't pay any attention to him." So he didn't pay attention, meaning he didn't speak to Mr. Lee. Then his mother stops him and gives him the advice about not passing someone without speaking. The options are A through D. Let's look at the choices: A. He talked with somebody else. The passage doesn't mention him talking to someone else at that moment. The mother was talking to Mr. Lee, but the author wasn't engaged. So maybe not A. B. He was attracted by a dog. The quote from the mother mentions a dog wagging its tail, but that's part of her lesson. The author doesn't say he saw a dog at that time. So B is probably not correct. C. He greeted him politely. But according to the passage, the author didn't pay any attention to Mr. Lee. Greeting politely would be the opposite of ignoring. So C is not correct. D. He ignored him. The passage says he didn't pay any attention to Mr. Lee when
